title: Court Rules Monitor Agent
shortDescription: Tracks rule changes, standing orders, and judge-specific procedures across the courts where your firm practices and notifies the affected matter teams.

workflow.label: Rule change published
workflow.detail: Local, circuit, or federal rule updates

workflow.label: Compare to ruleset

seo.title: AI Court Rules Monitor Agent for Law Firms | Dodonai
seo.description: Track rule changes, standing orders, and judge-specific procedures across every court your firm practices in. Notifies the affected matter teams automatically.

hero.headline: AI Court Rules Monitor Agent
hero.subheadline: Watches the courts your firm practices in for rule amendments, standing orders, and judge-specific procedure changes. Surfaces what changed, what it means, and which active matters are affected.
bullets: Per-court watchlist for rule amendments and standing orders
Active-matter impact analysis on every change
Plain-English summary delivered to the affected matter teams

scenario.heading: The standing order you would have missed
paragraphs: A federal judge in your district issues a new standing order requiring a 14-day pre-motion conference letter before any discovery motion. Your associate is 3 days from filing a motion to compel that would now violate the standing order. The Court Rules Monitor caught the order the morning it issued, identified the 6 of your firm's active matters in front of that judge, and sent each responsible attorney a plain-English summary with the matter list. The associate redrafts the motion plan; the conference letter goes out instead. No sanction, no embarrassed call to the client.

whatItDoes.heading: What the Court Rules Monitor does
bullets: Maintains a watchlist of every court and judge your firm practices in front of
Monitors rule amendments, standing orders, and procedure changes published by those courts
Identifies which of your active matters are affected by each change
Produces a plain-English summary of what changed and what it means
Routes the summary to the responsible matter teams in Slack or email

integrations: Court rule and standing order feeds (PACER, court websites, ECF notices)
Practice management system for the matter and venue list
Document management system for the summary archive

safety.heading: Safety posture
paragraphs: The summaries are explanatory; the action is the attorney's. The agent doesn't refile, withdraw, or amend; it surfaces what changed. The Managed retainer reviews the watchlist quarterly to confirm coverage matches your firm's active jurisdictions.

faq.question: What about the obscure local rule that isn't published online?
faq.answer: For courts where the rule changes aren't reliably online, the watchlist includes manual sources (clerk-of-court newsletters, bar association alerts) that the Managed retainer monitors directly. The agent reads what's automated; the team covers what isn't.

faq.question: Does this overlap with what our state bar emails us?
faq.answer: Bar emails cover statewide rule changes. The Court Rules Monitor adds the per-court and per-judge layer that bar emails don't, plus the matter-impact analysis that tells you which of your active matters need attention rather than which of your peers' might.
